signals.reasoning: The live SOL short has fully round-tripped from a real winner into a live loss, retained none of peak PnL, and is still flagged urgent. This is no longer a debate about thesis management; it is a hard cleanup exit.
BTC is strong enough on the day that fresh bearish continuation is no longer attractive here, especially after the live SOL short has already failed.
ETH is participating in the same recovery tape, so it does not offer a cleaner next action than simply flattening the open SOL risk.
TST remains far too extended and volatile to trust under an active event veto.
MERL is just another rebound name and does not offer a cleaner edge than getting out of the failed SOL short.
ORDI is recovering, but not with enough clarity to justify a fresh decision during a live macro headline regime.
CAKE is constructive but already well off the lows, making it a poor fresh trade while the board remains vetoed.
ZEREBRO remains noisy and high beta, so it is not where disciplined post-exit management should go.
TURBO has squeeze fuel, but it is still too unstable and too thin to trust under a live event veto.
WCT remains too extended and event-sensitive to justify touching after the SOL short failure.
VVV still looks like recovery rather than a clean fresh continuation and is not a better immediate decision than simply standing down.
